title: ageratina muelleri
intro: Ageratina muelleri, also known as Mexican mint or false pennyroyal, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly eugenol and thymol. The health benefits of Ageratina muelleri include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make topical creams and salves for skin issues like wounds and burns.
